questions.text: Receptor sites for neurotransmitters are present on the:
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Presynaptic membrane has synaptic vesicles which contain neurotransmitter. When nerve impulse reaches the vesicles, neurotransmitter is released into the synaptic cleft.
Tips of axons have synaptic vesicles, which contain neurotransmitter.

A neurotransmitter is a chemical substance responsible for the transmission of nerve impulses across a synapse. It is released by the synaptic vesicle into the synaptic cleft. The neurotransmitter binds with protein receptor molecule present on the postsynaptic membrane causing its depolarization and generation of an action potential.
Synaptic vesicles contain neurotransmitter for transmission of impulse.

questions.explanation: A neurotransmitter is a chemical substance responsible for the transmission of nerve impulses across a synapse. It is released by the synaptic vesicle into the synaptic cleft. The neurotransmitter binds with protein receptor molecule present on the postsynaptic membrane causing its depolarization and generation of an action potential.

questions.text: Good vision depends on adequate intake of carotene-rich food. Select the best option from the following state­ments:(1)Vitamin A derivatives are formed from carotene. (2)The photopigments are embedded in the membrane discs of the inner segment. (3)Retinal is a derivative of vitamin A. (4)Retinal is a light-absorbing part of all the visual photopigments.
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: 4th option is not related to the statement given above.

Beta carotene is a provitamin A carotenoid, or a nutrient that the body readily converts into vitamin A. Only 1 and 3 are related to the first statement, which emphasizes 'intake' and 'carotene-rich food'; 2 and 4 are not related to intake carotene carotene-rich food.
The 2nd and 4th options are not related to the statement given.
2nd option is not related to the statement above.

questions.explanation: Beta carotene is a provitamin A carotenoid, or a nutrient that the body readily converts into vitamin A. Hence, only 1 and 3 are related to the first statement emphasizes 'intake' and 'carotene-rich food'; 2 and 4 are not related to intake of carotene-rich food.

questions.text: Choose the correct statement.
questions.subject: Biology

options.text: Nociceptors respond to changes in pressure
options.explanation: Nociceptors respond to potentially damaging stimuli that result in pain.
Meissner's corpuscles are a type of mechanoreceptor, responsible for touch sensitivity.

options.text: Photoreceptors in the human eye are depolarised during darkness and become hyperpolarised in response to the light stimulus
options.explanation: Photoreceptors in the human eye are unique because they are the only type of sensory cells that are relatively depolarised (about –35mV) when it is at rest (i.e., in the dark), and hyperpolarised (to about –70mV) in response to adequate light stimulus.

options.text: Receptors do not produce graded potentials.
options.explanation: Receptors generally produce graded potentials called receptor potentials.

questions.explanation: Photoreceptors in the human eye are unique because they are the only type of sensory cells that are relatively depolarised (about –35mV) when it is at rest (i.e., in the dark), and hyperpolarised (to about –70mV) in response to adequate light stimulus. Nociceptors respond to potentially damaging stimuli that result in pain. Meissner’s corpuscles are a type of mechanoreceptor, responsible for touch sensitivity. Receptors generally produce graded potentials called receptor potentials.

questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Opsin is correct but not retinol.
Retinol also called vitamin A1 is needed for vision.
Guanosine is purine nucleoside have neuroprotective properties.Retinol also called vitamin A1 is needed for vision.

The rods contain a photosensitive pigment called rhodopsin.Rhodopsin is composed of opsin and retinene.The opsin is a protein and is called scotopsin in rhodopsin.The retinene is an aldehyde of vitamin A and is called retinal.

questions.explanation: The rods contain a photosensitive pigment called rhodopsin. Rhodopsin is composed of opsin and retinene.The opsin is a protein and is called scotopsin in rhodopsin.The retinene is an aldehyde of vitamin A and is called retinal.

questions.text: Destruction of the anterior horn cells of the spinal cord would result in the loss of:
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: "Commissural impulse" refers to the electrical signal that travels along nerve fibers (commissures) connecting the two cerebral hemispheres of the brain.

This is an incorrect option. In the brain, "integrating the impulse function" refers to the process by which a neuron combines numerous incoming electrical signals (postsynaptic potentials or PSPs) to determine whether or not to fire its own nerve impulse (action potential).
Sensory impulses are electrical signals that travel from sensory receptors to the central nervous system, carrying information about stimuli like touch, pain, and temperature.

The anterior horns of the spinal cord contain cells with fibres that form the anterior (motor) root end and are essential for the voluntary and reflex activity of muscles they innervate. If the anterior horn motor cells are destroyed, the nerves cannot regenerate impulses, and the muscles are never useful again.

questions.explanation: Peripheral motor nerves carry motor impulses from the spinal cord to voluntary muscles. The destruction of the cells of the anterior horn of the spinal cord will result in the loss of voluntary motor impulses. This is because the cells of the anterior horn (also called the anterior gray column), which is the front column of gray matter in the spinal cord, contain motor neurons that innervate the axillary muscles.

questions.text: In the mammalian eye, the ‘fovea’ is the center of the visual field where:
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: This is incorrect. Rods are concentrated in the periphery of the retina, not the fovea.

options.text: More rods than cones are found
options.explanation: This is incorrect. The fovea has a high density of cones, not rods.

options.text: High density of cones occurs, but it has no rods
options.explanation: A small oval, yellowish area of the retina lying exactly opposite to the centre of the cornea is named the macula lutea or yellow spot, which has at its middle a shallow depression, the fovea centralis. The fovea centralis has cone cells only. It is devoid of rods and blood vessels. The fovea centralis is the place of the most distinct vision.

options.text: An equal number of rods and cones
options.explanation: This is incorrect. The fovea is solely composed of cones, with no rods present.

questions.explanation: The fovea is the part of the retina with the highest concentration of cones, responsible for sharp central vision and color perception. Since cones are primarily used for detailed vision in bright light, the fovea has no rods, which are responsible for vision in low light conditions.

questions.text: A gymnast is able to balance his body upside down even in total darkness because of:
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: The tectorial membrane lies on top of the organ of Corti. The tectorial membrane is a critical structure in the inner ear that plays a vital role in hearing. It is an extracellular matrix that lies over the hair cells in the cochlea and helps stimulate them in response to sound waves.
It is the structure in the cochlea of the inner ear that produces nerve impulses in response to sound vibrations.
The vestibular apparatus forms the labyrinth of the inner ear in association with the cochlea.

The vestibular apparatus is present in the ear. It forms the labyrinth of the inner ear in association with the cochlea. This apparatus is responsible for the balance and sense of orientation of the body in the air. It consists of 3 semi-circular canals, the base of which consists of the ampulla, and the otolith organ, which consists of the utricle and saccule, which have a projecting ridge called the macula. When the body is oriented in a different form in the darkness or absence of light, the apparatus helps to determine the orientation. Thus, the correct answer is option D.

questions.explanation: The vestibular apparatus is present in the ear. It forms the labyrinth of the inner ear in association with the cochlea. This apparatus is responsible for the balance and sense of orientation of the body in the air. It consists of 3 semi-circular canals, the base of which consists of the ampulla, and the otolith organ, which consists of the utricle and saccule, which have a projecting ridge called the macula. When the body is oriented in a different form in the darkness or absence of light, the apparatus helps to determine the orientation. Thus, the correct answer is option D.

questions.text: Which of the following regions of the brain is incorrectly paired with its function?
questions.subject: Biology

options.text: Corpus callosum - communication between the left and right cerebral cortices
options.explanation: The corpus callosum facilitates communication between the left and right hemispheres of the brain, allowing for coordinated functions.
The cerebrum is responsible for higher cognitive functions, including calculation, contemplation, and other complex thought processes.
The medulla oblongata plays a crucial role in maintaining homeostasis by regulating vital functions such as heart rate and breathing.
The cerebellum is primarily involved in motor control and coordination, not language comprehension, which is a function of the cerebrum. This makes it the incorrect pairing.

questions.explanation: The cerebellum coordinates movement and balance. Language comprehension is a function of the cerebrum.

questions.text: Injury localized to the hypothalamus would most likely disrupt:
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Short-term memory is mainly associated with the hippocampus in the temporal lobe, not the hypothalamus.

Coordination during locomotion is controlled mainly by the cerebellum, not the hypothalamus. The cerebellum integrates sensory input with motor commands to maintain balance, posture, and smooth, coordinated movements while walking or running.

options.text: Executive functions; such as decision making
options.explanation: Executive functions such as decision-making, planning, and judgment are primarily controlled by the frontal lobe of the cerebrum, especially the prefrontal cortex. The hypothalamus, on the other hand, mainly regulates autonomic functions like hunger, thirst, temperature, and hormone release, not higher executive tasks.

The hypothalamus lies at the base of the thalamus. It provides an anatomical connection between the nervous and endocrine systems through its relationship to the pituitary gland. The hypothalamus is a thermoregulatory centre. Hence, it is called the “thermostat” of the body. It keeps body temperature at roughly 37°C by means of a complex thermostat system. Any localised injury to the hypothalamus will, hence, disrupt the regulation of body temperature.

questions.explanation: The hypothalamus lies at the base of the thalamus. It provides an anatomical connection between the nervous and endocrine systems through its relationship to the pituitary gland. The hypothalamus is a thermoregulatory centre. Hence, it is called the “thermostat” of the body. It keeps body temperature at roughly 37°C by means of a complex thermostat system. Any localised injury to the hypothalamus will, hence, disrupt the regulation of body temperature.

questions.text: Which one of the following statements is not correct?
questions.subject: Biology

options.text: Retinal is the light absorbing portion of visual photo pigments.
options.explanation: Retinal is the chemical basis of visual phototransduction, light detection stage of visual perception.Hence this statement is correct.

options.text: In the retina, the rods have the photopigment rhodopsin while cones have three different photopigments.
options.explanation: Rods have rhodopsin photopigments while cones have opsin photopigments for red, green and blue colours.

options.text: Retinal is a derivative of vitamin C
options.explanation: The retinal pigment is an aldehyde of vitamin A.

options.text: Rhodopsin is the purplish red protein present in rods only
options.explanation: This statement is factually correct as only rods have rhodopsin.

questions.explanation: The retinal pigment is an aldehyde of vitamin A.

questions.text: Parts A, B, C and D of the human eye are shown in the diagram.Select the option which gives correct identification along with its functions/characteristics:
questions.subject: Biology

options.text: C-­Aqueous chamber - ­Reflects the light which does not pass through the lens
options.explanation: C is aqueous humour. It nourishes the cornea and lens both of which are avascular.

options.text: D- Choroid ­- Its anterior part forms the ciliary body
options.explanation: D is the sclera. It is the outermost covering and maintains the shape of an eyeball. It also protects the inner layers of the eye.

options.text: A - Retina -Contains photoreceptors i.e.rods and cones.
options.explanation: A is the retina which is the innermost layer, containing photoreceptors rods and cones.

options.text: B-Blind spot - ­Has only a few rods and cones.
options.explanation: . B is the blind spot. Optic nerves pierce through the retina at the blind spot. It has no visual cells.

questions.explanation: In the given figure, A is the retina which is the innermost layer, containing photoreceptors rods and cones. B is the blind spot. Optic nerves pierce through the retina at the blind spot. It has no visual cells. C is aqueous humour. It nourishes the cornea and lens both of which are avascular. D is the sclera. It is the outermost covering and maintains the shape of an eyeball. It also protects the inner layers of the eye.

questions.text: The figure shows an axon terminal and synapse. Select the option giving correct identification of labels A to ­D.
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: A is action potential but C is receptor. An action potential is a rapid electrical change in a neuron’s membrane that transmits a nerve impulse. A receptor is a structure that detects a stimulus and generates a nerve impulse.

B is neurotransmitter but D is synaptic vesicle. A neurotransmitter is a chemical messenger that transmits signals between neurons across a synapse. A synaptic vesicle is a tiny sac that stores and releases neurotransmitters at the synapse.

This is the correct option as C shows the receptor which receives the nerve impulse and D shows the synaptic vesicles which release neurotransmitter, helping to propagate the impulse aling the synapse.

Incorrect because A is action potential and B is neurotransmitter. Axon terminal is the forms the presynaptic membrane and brings the impulse towards the synapse. Serotonin complex is a type of neurotransmitter.

questions.explanation: A is action potential, B is neurotransmitter, C is receptor and D is synaptic vesicles. An action potential is a rapid electrical change in a neuron’s membrane that transmits a nerve impulse. A neurotransmitter is a chemical messenger that transmits signals between neurons across a synapse. A receptor is a structure that detects a stimulus and generates a nerve impulse. A synaptic vesicle is a tiny sac that stores and releases neurotransmitters at the synapse.

questions.text: Select the correct option.
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Eustachian tube equalizes air pressure within the tympanic cavity.
The cerebellum is a part of hind hindbrain. It is involved in muscular coordination, balance, and the position of the body in space.

The hypothalamus is the region of the forebrain in the floor of the third ventricle, linked with the thalamus above and the pituitary gland below. It contains several important centres controlling body temperature, thirst, hunger, and eating, water balance, and sexual function. It is also closely connected with emotional activity and sleep, and functions as a centre for the integration of hormonal and autonomic nervous activity through its control of the pituitary secretions.
This option is incorrect as the blind spot does not have rods and cones, which contribute to colour and vision.

questions.explanation: The hypothalamus is the region of the forebrain in the floor of the third ventricle, linked with the thalamus above and the pituitary gland below. It contains several important centres controlling body temperature, thirst, hunger, and eating, water balance, and sexual function. It is also closely connected with emotional activity and sleep, and functions as a centre for the integration of hormonal and autonomic nervous activity through its control of the pituitary secretions.

questions.text: Which part of human brain is concerned with the regulation of body temperature?
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Your cerebellum is a part of your brain located at the back of your head, just above and behind where your spinal cord connects to your brain itself. It is involved in muscular coordination,balance and position of body in space.

Cerebrum, the largest and uppermost portion of the brain. The cerebrum consists of the cerebral hemispheres and accounts for two-thirds of the total weight of the brain. One hemisphere, usually the left, is functionally dominant, controlling language and speech.
Your hypothalamus, a structure deep in your brain, acts as your body's smart control coordinating center. Its main function is to keep your body in a stable state called homeostasis.
Medulla oblongata is the reflex center of vital tasks like heart beat, breathing, sneezing, vomiting and coughing.

questions.explanation: Your hypothalamus, a structure deep in your brain, acts as your body's smart control coordinating center. Its main function is to keep your body in a stable state called homeostasis.The hypothalamus controls body temperature by acting like a thermostat, receiving information from heat and cold sensors throughout the body to maintain a core temperature of about 37°C (98.6°F). If the body is too cold, it triggers mechanisms like vasoconstriction, shivering, and increased metabolism to generate and retain heat. If the body is too hot, it activates cooling responses such as vasodilation (widening of skin blood vessels) and sweating to release heat into the environment.

questions.text: Cornea transplant in humans is rarely rejected.This is because:
questions.subject: Biology

options.text: It is composed of enucleated cells
Its cells are least penetrable by bacteria

options.explanation: The cornea is a transparent portion that forms the anterior one-sixth of the eyeball. The cornea admits and helps to focus light waves as they enter the eye. The cornea is avascular (i.e., has no blood supply). This part of the eye absorbs oxygen from the air. The cornea was one of the first organs to be successfully transplanted because it lacks blood vessels.

questions.explanation: The cornea is a transparent portion that forms the anterior one-sixth of the eyeball. The cornea admits and helps to focus light waves as they enter the eye. The cornea is avascular (i.e., has no blood supply). This part of the eye absorbs oxygen from the air. The cornea was one of the first organs to be successfully transplanted because it lacks blood vessels.

questions.text: Bowman’s glands are located in the:
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: The anterior pituitary is the front lobe of your pituitary gland, which is a small, pea-sized gland located at the base of your brain below your hypothalamus.

options.text: Female reproductive system of the cockroach
options.explanation: The reproductive system of a female cockroach consists of two large ovaries, genital chamber, vagina, spermatheca, colleterial gland, external genitalia and oviduct.

The bowman’s gland, also called the olfactory gland is any of the branched tubuloalveolar glands situated in the mucous membrane of the olfactory region of the nasal cavity that produces mucus to moisten the olfactory epithelium and dissolve odour containing gases.
No. This is incorrect.
